verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic/mitochondrial pro-apoptotic protein that inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane upon activation. However, during apoptosis and immunogenic cell death, BAX translocates to and oligomerizes in the mitochondrial outer membrane and, importantly, has documented surface exposure on the plasma membrane outer leaflet of apoptotic and stressed cells. Multiple studies show BAX ecto-display on tumor and dying cells, making it a documented cell-state-induced surface target. This qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced even though the baseline localization is cytosolic/mitochondrial.
